question: I'm a first-time B2B founder pre-PMF. Most prioritization frameworks assume an established feature set and a big company. Is there a framework that works for an early-stage startup still hunting for product-market fit?
How do you prioritize your roadmap? Which prioritization framework do you recommend for a team within a mid-sized company?
What are your favorite decision-making frameworks for hard, one-way-door decisions?
My team keeps trying to launch a big bet project at a big company, but our resources keep getting pulled away. How do I keep support for a long-horizon bet?
I'm struggling to decide whether to sunset a feature that's eating a lot of our engineering resources. How do I make that call?
Should I always run an experiment when I make a change to my product? If not, when should I skip the test?
What's your favorite roadmap template that you like to use?
Do you have examples of great 1-Pagers, PRDs, or product specs? I want a structure I can borrow and a sense of what great looks like.
How do you think about setting goals for your team and company each quarter?
I've heard podcast guests mention first-principles thinking. What exactly is it and how do I use it in my work?
We have a backlog of 40 ideas and 6 engineers. How do I objectively rank them when stakeholders all think their request is the top priority?
I have a three-step process I want for solving any thorny product problem from scratch. What's a repeatable method?
When creating a new product, what strategies minimize the cold start problem — making it useful with zero users and zero data?
Can you go one level deeper on the Racecar Growth Framework — how do I actually use it in practice?
My product is growing, but slowly. What most often precedes an inflection in growth?
Can you share examples of companies that found innovative growth loops, like marketplaces getting supply to drive demand?
I noticed some of my customers are signing up to become supply in my marketplace, which grows it faster. Any advice for leaning into this?
I'm thinking about my growth strategy. Beyond Facebook ads and SEO, what are all the ways people discover new products?
I loved the consumer growth series, but are there other growth tactics or levers founders have found effective?
I'm looking for creative ways to get the word out about my product. Do you have examples of clever growth tactics for inspiration?
Which startups are best at content-driven growth, and what can I learn from them?
This Justin Kan tweet about distribution stuck with me — how do I find my product's distribution advantage?
I work at a fintech company where the core challenge is building trust and we're not a daily-use product. How should I think about growth here?
We're a startup struggling with growth and retention because of an economic downturn. Any advice on what we should do?
I want to build a referral program for my marketplace. Any advice — I haven't found great content on this?
I'm planning to launch my product soon. How do companies generate buzz when they launch?
How do I hit #1 on Product Hunt, and how do I know if a Product Hunt launch is even right for my startup?
I'm an early-stage marketplace and need to kickstart supply for a labor marketplace like Thumbtack. How do labor marketplaces find their supply?
What are the 28 ways to grow supply in a marketplace? I need a checklist of supply-side acquisition tactics.
Our self-serve signup rate is fine but only 28% of signups ever reach the aha moment. How do I systematically improve activation?
Our weekly active users have been flat at ~12k for five months despite shipping features. How do I diagnose where my growth engine is stalling?
I'm thinking about expanding my marketplace business internationally. What should I be thinking about when making and executing this decision?
How do marketplaces expand from a single market to new markets? When should we expand and how do we pick which markets to enter?
How do strategy, vision, mission, goals, and roadmap all fit together? Where should I start?
How do I get better at product strategy?
How do I know if I should pivot or stay the course?
I keep hearing I need a wedge to narrow our product and GTM. What exactly is a wedge and how do I pick the right one?
I know differentiation matters, but why exactly, and what are concrete ways we can differentiate our product?
How can I differentiate my product and business from potential competitors in the eyes of our customers?
I'm working on a startup idea — what types of business models exist, and are there creative ways to design our business model?
I've worked on my startup for two years and it isn't taking off. What are my options?
I'm told my startup idea isn't venture-scale. What does that actually mean and how do I assess it?
As an investor, how do you evaluate a marketplace business idea? What do you look for?
I'm building a marketplace and want to know what's most likely to kill my business. What should I watch out for?
How do marketplaces win? What do new marketplaces need to get right to succeed?
I'm building a new marketplace. How do I build trust between strangers transacting for the first time?
We have a strategy doc but the team can't remember it. How do I structure strategy so it's memorable and actually drives decisions?
Our enterprise competitor just shipped the feature we were betting on as our differentiator. How do I rethink our strategic position?
I have a startup idea and I've started talking to potential customers. What should I be asking them to know if I have product-market fit?
How do I know if my product is working? What does it feel like when you've found product-market fit?
I'm working on a marketplace startup. How long does it usually take to find product-market fit?
I'm a B2B founder. Is there a specific guide for finding product-market fit in B2B, since most PMF advice feels consumer-focused?
I have an idea for a startup. How can I validate it's a good idea, and how will I know when it's promising enough to commit to?
How did today's fastest-growing B2B startups turn their early users into paying customers?
We ran the Sean Ellis survey and only 28% of users said they'd be very disappointed without us. Are we close to PMF and what do we fix?
We're well funded and have been trying to scale a product but it's not working. We're under board pressure. How do I think about whether we have PMF?

What KPIs should I focus on and track for a marketplace?
I'm building a consumer app and wondering what metrics I should focus on.

What is considered a good activation rate?
I learned a lot from your activation rate post, but how do I quantitatively determine my activation milestone for a multiplayer B2B SaaS product?
What is a good monthly churn for a SaaS business?
What do you consider good and great retention?
What is considered a good free-to-paid conversion rate?
What is a good waitlist conversion, and any advice for making the most of our waitlist?
What growth rates do investors expect at each stage of the business?
You talk about retention a lot, but how do I actually measure cohort retention? I haven't found a good guide.
You've mentioned regression analysis a few times. What exactly is a regression analysis and how do I run one?

We send customers a monthly report card but execs keep saying the metrics aren't 'actionable.' How do I make a metrics report genuinely actionable?
I'm a PM having a hard time getting buy-in for my ideas — teammates shoot me down or disregard my recommendations. Any advice?
I know that as a PM I need to lead through influence. How do I get better at influence?

I often have to say no to my manager. What's an effective technique for saying no while keeping their trust and not being seen as the 'no' person?
Our MVP launched and is doing horribly. In an old-fashioned environment where sharing failure isn't common, how do I communicate this to execs?

How do I get buy-in from leadership to empower product teams, when sales expects fully scoped projects and there's no design resourcing?
I need to write a one-pager to get exec approval for a risky bet. How should I structure the argument so leadership says yes?
How did Airbnb balance outcome-thinking with design and technical requirements across teams?
I'm trying to decide if we should switch to a GM model. When and why do companies make this move, and any tips for the transition?
My startup grew to four PMs and I feel us getting out of sync. Any advice on running a recurring PM team meeting?
I'm joining a team with a history of underperformance and execution issues. Any advice for a turnaround situation?
I constantly feel like my team is moving too slowly. How can I help my team move faster?
My engineering team is dragging its feet on deadlines that aren't tied to anything specific. How do I motivate them to hit deadlines?
I find it difficult to keep designers and engineers excited about driving metrics. How do I align everyone around outcomes?
As a manager I'm worried about giving people months off. How do I structure a sabbatical program that works for the person and the company?

I'm having a hard time working with the designer on my team. Any advice for working with designers?
What's one thing you've changed within your organization that made your team meaningfully more effective?
Our startup is growing quickly. When should we hire our first product manager?
When is the right time to hire my first full-time growth person, and what should I look for when hiring for growth?
How do I hire and build my first growth team?
I'm a founder of a small startup and I'm having a really hard time hiring. What works best for startups?
How should I structure my PM interview process?
What are your favorite interview questions when interviewing senior product managers?
What's the single best interview question to learn the most about a candidate?
Work trials keep coming up on your podcast. I'm sold — what advice do you have for setting up a successful work trial process?
I have to write a job description for the first time and every one I read is boring. Have you seen any that stand out?
What should interviewers look for when hiring a Director of Product?
I raised a small seed round and I'm now thinking about raising our next round. Any advice for navigating the process?
We're a three-person SaaS startup onboarding a few customers a week. We've raised angel money — when is it right to raise more?
I'm raising money and keep getting asked 'Why now?' I don't have a great answer. How important is it to have a why-now?
I want to get into angel investing. How does one get started, and what surprised you most about it?
We're burning $80k/month with 9 months of runway and growing 15% MoM. Should we raise now or push for stronger metrics first?
An investor asked what our milestones are for this round. How do I size a round and tie it to milestones we can credibly hit?
I have a self-serve bottom-up SaaS product and I'm trying to decide if, when, and how to hire my first full-time salesperson.
I'm building a B2B SaaS startup and trying to decide which market segment to target and whether to go product-led or sales-led. Any advice?
A lot is written about driving traffic but not converting it. What best practices had the most impact on conversion at Airbnb?
I want to increase the conversion rate of our core flow. Beyond the usual advice, what else can I try?
What's the advice for introducing growth to a company that still thinks of it as just 'sales and marketing'?
We're a PLG product getting inbound from a few 500-person companies. How do I know it's time to add a sales motion and what's the first step?
I'm building a SaaS product and don't know where to start with pricing. How should I approach my pricing strategy?
How do I figure out how to price my product?
I'm building a marketplace and struggling to pick my take rate. How do I decide what percentage to charge?
When does it make sense to make my product free, and how do I decide between a free trial and freemium?
What trends are you seeing in how incumbents price AI features and products? Any innovation in how companies charge?
I'm building a consumer subscription app and want a durable B2C subscription business. What does it take?
We charge $15/month flat but heavy users get far more value than light users. Should we move to usage-based or seat-based pricing?
I have an idea for a startup. How do I run customer interviews to learn whether the problem is real before building anything?
How do I develop product sense?
I want to do continuous discovery but my team only talks to customers when a project kicks off. How do I build an ongoing discovery habit?
We get tons of feature requests in support tickets and sales calls. How do I synthesize that raw input into real product opportunities?
I keep hearing I need to improve my product's positioning but I'm not sure what that means. Where do I start?

I'm just starting out as a PM. What habits should I be building early on to become great?
I'm overwhelmed with work. Do you have productivity tips that help you stay above water?
I've been stuck at the same level for a few years. What advice do you have for getting promoted?
I recently transitioned into a PM role and never know if I'm doing a good job. Day to day, how can I tell?

I'm interviewing for a PM role. What should I do to prepare?
I'm about to start interviewing and I'm bad at negotiating comp. How do I learn to negotiate without pissing anyone off?
I fear layoffs are coming at my company. What can I do to prepare in case I get laid off?

I'm finding it tough to work with my manager. Do you have any advice for managing up?
How do I make the transition from a regular PM to a growth PM, especially without a technical background?
